description: Machine-readable registry of all UI skills in this repository.

distribution.note: All skills support all install methods below. Universal layout is a local export path, not a published skills.sh registry entry.

skills.name: ui-surface-inventory
skills.description: Map an existing app into coherent scopes and surfaces before any spec work begins.
skills.input: existing app (URL, screenshots, or source code)
skills.output: UI scope map with surfaces, sub-surfaces, and recovery order
skills.workflow_position: first — always run before ui-brief on an existing product

skills.name: ui-brief
skills.description: Turn a vague UI idea into a structured product and design brief.
skills.input: rough feature idea or product description
skills.output: brief.md — goal, primary user, success criteria, constraints, non-goals
skills.workflow_position: step 1 of the core spec workflow

skills.name: ui-visual-calibration
skills.description: Translate vague visual preferences into concrete layout and visual language decisions.
skills.input: approved brief + visual references or aesthetic preferences
skills.output: visual-calibration.md — density, shape, surface style, palette guidance, typography
skills.workflow_position: step 2 of the core spec workflow

skills.name: ui-flow
skills.description: Map the user journey and decision points across a multi-screen feature.
skills.output: flow.md — annotated screen-by-screen journey with transitions and decision points
skills.workflow_position: optional parallel step after ui-brief; run before ui-blueprint for multi-screen features

skills.name: ui-blueprint
skills.description: Convert an approved brief into a concrete layout and visual blueprint.
skills.output: blueprint.md — layout archetype, information hierarchy, text wireframe, responsive behaviour
skills.workflow_position: step 3 of the core spec workflow

skills.name: ui-system
skills.description: Define the design token system — colours, typography, spacing, shape, shadow.
skills.workflow_position: step 4 of the core spec workflow

skills.name: ui-screen-spec
skills.description: Map components to screen regions and define data dependencies and state ownership.
skills.output: screen-spec.md — region map, component assignments, data dependencies, state ownership
skills.workflow_position: step 5 of the core spec workflow

skills.name: ui-component-spec
skills.description: Specify a single interactive component exhaustively — anatomy, props, states, validation, accessibility.
skills.input: approved screen spec (or approved blueprint for standalone components)
skills.workflow_position: step 6 of the core spec workflow (per component)

skills.name: ui-microcopy
skills.description: Write or refine UX copy — button labels, error messages, empty states, tooltips.
skills.input: approved component specs or screen spec
skills.output: microcopy.md — copy for every interactive element, state, and edge case
skills.workflow_position: step 7 of the core spec workflow

skills.name: ui-acceptance
skills.description: Convert an approved spec into concrete, testable acceptance criteria.
skills.output: acceptance.md — machine-checkable criteria for every state and behaviour
skills.workflow_position: step 8 of the core spec workflow

skills.name: ui-spec-linter
skills.description: Validate a complete spec package for completeness, vocabulary, and state coverage before code generation.

skills.name: ui-generate-code
skills.description: Generate production-ready component code from an approved and linted spec package.
skills.input: approved spec package + clean lint report
skills.output: component source files, tests, and Storybook stories

skills.name: ui-inspector
skills.description: Gather DOM inventory, computed styles, token usage, accessibility findings, and responsiveness data from a live or static implementation.
skills.input: live URL, screenshot, or source code

skills.name: ui-redline
skills.description: Diagnose an existing UI implementation against an approved spec, rank mismatches, and prescribe exact fixes.
skills.input: approved spec package + inspector report (or screenshot/code snippet for quick reviews)
skills.output: redlines/redline-audit.md — ranked mismatches with prescribed fixes and a refactor prompt
skills.workflow_position: step 12 — always run after ui-inspector for formal audits

skills.name: ui-docs-sync
skills.description: Verify that repository documentation, product contracts, route docs, and UI spec packages are linked and consistent.
skills.workflow_position: after creating or updating a spec package, to keep repo docs in sync

skills.name: ui-agent-routing
skills.description: Wire a completed spec package into AI-agent-facing routing files so coding agents discover and use the active spec before editing implementation code.
skills.input: completed or accepted spec package + agent-facing routing files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, llm-docs/, etc.)
skills.output: routing report with exact edits or applied patches; DEPRECATED.md in superseded spec folders; updated 00-index.md discovery section
skills.workflow_position: final activation step — run after ui-docs-sync to make the spec operationally discoverable by AI agents

skills.name: setup-interface-skills
skills.description: Configure a repository for Interface Skills, including folders, agent routing, and root policy.
skills.output: INTERFACE_SKILLS.md, spec folders, and patched agent routing files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, etc.)
skills.workflow_position: initial setup — run once per repository before any other skills

skills.name: ui-spec-reconcile
skills.description: Reconcile a UI spec package with the current implementation after fixes or audits.
skills.input: spec package + redline report or code evidence
skills.workflow_position: after code fixes or redline audits to stabilize the spec as source of truth

skills.name: ui-to-issues
skills.description: Slice a UI spec package or redline audit into independently implementable issues.
skills.input: spec package, redline audit, or acceptance checklist
skills.output: markdown issue drafts grouped by vertical UI slices
skills.workflow_position: after spec approval or redline audit to plan implementation work

skills.name: ui-storybook-docs
skills.description: Generate or update Storybook MDX documentation from a component spec.
skills.input: approved component spec + generated component code

skills.name: ui-orchestrator
skills.description: Analyse a spec package, identify the current state and gaps, and recommend the next skill to run.
skills.input: spec package directory (any state of completion)
skills.output: gap analysis + skill recommendation + suggested prompt
skills.workflow_position: meta-skill — can be run at any point; tells you where you are
next: any skill based on gap analysis
